beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home while listening to rain on the roof.
beats.reasoning: Early rise on a grey, drizzly morning; Felix tends to pace days around weather and mood, so a quiet start suits the rainy forecast and his introverted nature.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby bagel shop for breakfast and strong opinions on their bagel quality.
beats.location_name: Beacon Hill Bagel & Coffee (Beacon Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Felix has strong opinions about bagels and an adventurous palate; a neighborhood walk errand before work fits his routine, and the rain won't stop a short local trip.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill office, avoiding SR-520 closure by taking I-5 south, adding buffer time for rain and congestion.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Felix commutes from Beacon Hill to First Hill (south of downtown), so I-5 is the natural route and avoids the closure entirely. Rain adds 10–15 min to normal commute.

beats.activity: Freelance photography session indoors: portrait/product shoot for a local client, staying dry during peak rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain from 7am–2pm makes outdoor photography impractical; Felix pivots to an indoor studio session on First Hill, staying productive while the weather is poor.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a nearby café, rain tapering off, catching up on social media and emails.
beats.reasoning: Rain is ending by 2pm; a sheltered lunch on-site makes sense, and Felix uses the break to reset before afternoon work or bartending prep.

beats.activity: Return to freelance work: edit and cull photos from morning session, file management, and admin tasks.
beats.reasoning: Post-lunch productivity push while still on-site; Felix wraps up the day's photography work before shifting to his part-time bartending shift.

beats.activity: Commute to bar in Capitol Hill, rain cleared, light traffic as evening settles in.
beats.reasoning: Across-town drive from First Hill to Capitol Hill (45 min budget); rain is done by now, so Felix can head to his part-time bartending gig with normal commute time.

beats.activity: Bartend shift: mix drinks, chat with regulars, manage the bar during dinner and evening crowd.
beats.reasoning: Felix's part-time bartending gig; Tuesday evening is moderate energy, allowing him to interact with close regulars (he values close friends) while avoiding big crowds.

beats.activity: Commute home to Beacon Hill, decompressing from shift, stopping for a late-night snack or tea.
beats.reasoning: Across-town drive back to Beacon Hill from Capitol Hill (45 min); Felix takes his time, allowing buffer for late-night traffic and a mental wind-down.

beats.activity: Unwind at home: video gaming session, perhaps a quick stargazing glance if clouds break, then preparing for bed.
beats.reasoning: Felix's introverted nature and love of gaming and stargazing suit a quiet evening; rain has cleared by night, so there's a small chance for stars before sleep.
A full 6-hour sleep cycle ending at 6:30am, allowing Felix to recover from a long work day and rest before tomorrow's schedule.

diary: Woke to that perfect sound of rain drumming on the roof—felt like the city was giving me permission to move slow. Grabbed my usual at Beacon Hill Bagel, though I swear their everything bagels have been inconsistent lately. The SR-520 closure had me paranoid about timing, so I left early and hit I-5 instead. Spent the morning doing a product shoot for a local startup; something about staying dry indoors while the weather hammered outside felt productive. Café Solstice for lunch, scrolling through emails between bites. The afternoon was admin work—culling photos, organizing files, the unglamorous side of freelancing that nobody talks about. By the time I got to The Crooked Table, the rain had cleared and I was ready to be around people. Busy shift, good tips, the usual mix of regulars and tourists. Stopped for tea on the way home and just sat with it, feeling that pleasant exhaustion. Played some games before bed, never did see the stars.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure pushed me toward structure—I planned my route early and chose the indoor shoot over any outdoor work, which felt like the smart call. There was something grounding about the grey morning that made me want coffee and consistency. By midday, cabin fever was setting in from screens and editing, so lunch felt like a real break. My mood shifted when I left First Hill; the cleared skies and lighter traffic felt like permission to transition into bartending mode. I wanted human interaction after hours of solo work. The evening felt balanced—I'm usually drained after shifts, but tonight it was the good kind of tired, the kind that comes from being present with people rather than just pushing through tasks.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
